title: Claim Ledger
description: Append-only run-level ledger maintained by the orchestrator. Every claim emitted by any sub-agent is appended here with a hash-chain link, producing an immutable audit trail. The head hash is periodically anchored to a separate R2 bucket for tamper detection.

ended_at.description: Set when the deliverable is rendered or the run is aborted. Open ledgers (no ended_at) indicate in-flight runs.

position.description: 0-indexed. Strictly monotonic — no gaps, no out-of-order appends.

kind.description: Event type. Every kind has a corresponding payload shape; the schema does not enforce per-kind payload shapes (those are documented in plugin code) to keep the ledger schema fast to validate.

actor.description: Which sub-agent or system component appended this entry.

payload.description: Event-specific payload. Shape varies by kind; not constrained at this schema level.

prev_hash.description: Hash of the previous entry. For position=0, the value is 'sha256:0000...0000' (32 zero bytes), reserved for genesis.

this_hash.description: SHA-256(prev_hash || canonical_json(position, kind, ts, actor, payload)). Recomputable from prior entries; mismatch indicates tampering.

entries.description: Append-only. Validation rejects any operation that mutates an existing entry.

head_hash.description: Hash of the most recent entry. Used as the anchor value when the ledger is periodically snapshotted to an external R2 bucket for tamper resistance.

external_bucket.description: R2 bucket name where head_hash snapshots are stored.

snapshot_key.description: S3-style key of the snapshot object.

anchor.description: Optional. Most ledgers are anchored on a periodic schedule rather than per-event.
